title: Brent crude hits 4-year high as US weighs new strikes on Iran
summary: Fox News reports Brent crude oil prices spiked to a four-year high of $126 per barrel earlier on April 30 amid reports that the US is considering new military strikes on Iran, before easing slightly later in the trading day.
contents:
# Brent crude hits 4-year high as US weighs new strikes on Iran

## Market Move
Fox News breaking-news coverage cited global market data showing Brent crude oil prices hit $126 per barrel earlier on Thursday April 30, the highest level in over four years, before paring some gains during the trading day. The spike followed news that CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper would brief President Trump on April 30 with new kinetic options against Iran, including a potential special operations forces raid against Iran's highly enriched uranium stockpile.

## Backdrop
The US naval blockade in effect since April 13 has redirected 42 vessels from Iranian ports and stranded 41 tankers carrying 69 million barrels of Iranian crude worth roughly $6 billion. Fox News coverage emphasised the economic consequences for US consumers, anticipated impact on midwestern refineries that previously processed light Iranian crude alternatives, and pressure on Saudi Arabia and the UAE to increase output.

## Political Reaction
The White House did not comment on specific kinetic options ahead of the Cooper briefing. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on April 30 publicly characterised the blockade as illegal and predicted operational failure, framing US measures as destabilising rather than secured.
